programs.name: Apple Health Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, copays for low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers Part A/B fully; SLMB/QI cover premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees in WA with incomes/assets below limits (e.g., QMB: 100% FPL).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Reduces out-of-pocket Medicare costs; automatic QMB eligibility for full Medicaid.

programs.name: Tailored Supports for Older Adults (TSOA)
programs.what_it_does: TSOA offers long-term care services in private homes for those requiring Nursing Facility Level of Care. It uniquely allows higher asset limits than standard Medicaid waivers.[1]
programs.who_its_for: WA residents 55+ needing nursing-level care, living at home; asset limit $84,354 (2026).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Home-based long-term services/supports without full Medicaid financial eligibility.[1]

programs.name: Medicaid Personal Care (MPC)
programs.what_it_does: MPC provides in-home personal care assistance with ADLs/IADLs like bathing, dressing, meals, and housework. Participants can self-direct care via individual providers.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Elderly or disabled WA residents not needing nursing home care, Medicaid-eligible.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Entitlement program with no waitlist; services in home, adult family home, or assisted living.[2]

programs.name: New Freedom Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Participant-directed program gives individualized budgets for in-home services to prevent nursing home placement. Supports include personal care, home mods, meals, emergency systems.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Elderly (65+) or disabled (18-64) in select counties (e.g., King, Pierce, Spokane) at risk of institutionalization.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Custom budget for independence-promoting services; administered by DSHS ALTSA.[4]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE (federal program administered locally) delivers comprehensive medical and social services to maintain community living. Includes adult day health, home care, and hospitalization if needed.
programs.who_its_for: WA seniors 55+ certified for nursing home level care, able to live safely in community.
programs.benefit_value: Capitated Medicare/Medicaid coverage; prevents institutionalization (limited WA sites).

programs.name: Basic Food Program
programs.what_it_does: WA's SNAP provides monthly EBT benefits for groceries and food purchases. Expands access for seniors via combined apps.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income WA seniors 60+; higher asset disregard for elderly/disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200+/month per person; no time limit for ABAWD seniors.

programs.name: Housing and Essential Needs (HEN) Referral Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers financial assistance for essential needs like rent, utilities, transportation, home repairs, food, and personal health items. Helps reduce overall costs including indirect medical expenses.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and families meeting income and disability requirements.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Washington state-funded program providing flexible cash assistance for a wide range of housing and essential needs beyond standard federal aid.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Varies based on needs; covers rent, utilities, transportation, repairs, and more.[1]

programs.name: Washington Energy Assistance Program (WEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP-funded grants help low-income households pay heating costs. Prioritizes elderly/disabled.
programs.who_its_for: WA households <150% FPL with seniors/disabled members.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Free home energy upgrades (insulation, sealing, efficient appliances) reduce utility bills. Administered via WA Commerce.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income WA households, priority for seniors/disabled.

programs.name: Health Insurance Counseling and Advocacy Program (HICAP/SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Free Medicare counseling, plan comparisons, appeals help for WA seniors. Operated via Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: All WA Medicare beneficiaries, especially low-income.
programs.benefit_value: Personalized assistance saving thousands in premiums/costs.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals (via Aging & Disability Resources)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote independence. Often OAA-funded through AAAs.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound WA seniors 60+ unable to shop/prepare meals.

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, training, counseling for caregivers of seniors. Includes short-term relief services.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of WA seniors 60+.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Part-time paid training jobs in community service for low-income seniors. Leads to unsubsidized employment.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed WA seniors 55+ at 125% FPL or below.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work 20 hrs/week; supportive services.

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors (via Protection & Advocacy)
programs.what_it_does: Free legal help for elder abuse, benefits, housing issues. Provided by WA Long-Term Care Ombudsman/Legal Aid orgs.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income WA seniors 60+ facing legal barriers.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living resolving complaints. Protects rights in LTC facilities.
programs.who_its_for: WA seniors in long-term care facilities.

programs.name: Washington Charity Care
programs.what_it_does: Provides free or discounted healthcare services including hospital charges, doctor's visits, medications, and X-rays. Eligible individuals can apply at any time, even for previously paid bills or those in collections.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors within 300% of the federal poverty level.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific charity care law offering half-price or full coverage on a broad range of medical services not universally available as a state program elsewhere.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free or discounted healthcare services up to full coverage on qualifying medical costs.[1]

programs.name: Aged, Blind, or Disabled (ABD) Cash Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides cash to cover monthly bills, food, and housing essentials. Supplements Social Security for those unable to work due to health reasons, aiding medical costs.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ who are aged, blind, or disabled and unable to work.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State cash assistance program tailored for non-working seniors supplementing federal benefits, not a standard federal program.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Monthly cash payments for essentials like food and housing.[1]

programs.name: WA Cares Fund
programs.what_it_does: Provides access to long-term care services and supports for working Washingtonians. Allows flexible choice of covered services to meet individual needs.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Working Washington residents earning long-term care benefits.[6]
programs.whats_unique: State-mandated long-term care insurance fund unique to Washington, offering comprehensive coverage beyond federal Medicare.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Flexible long-term care services and supports based on earned benefits.[6]
